heading: Canaveral National Seashore Advisory Commission
label: 459j–5 Canaveral National Seashore Advisory Commission
notes: Amendments1976—Subsec. (a). substituted “six members” for “five members” in introductory provisions and substituted “two” for “one” and inserted requirement relating to residency of each member in cl. (3).Pub. L. 94–398
amendments: Amendments1976—Subsec. (a). substituted “six members” for “five members” in introductory provisions and substituted “two” for “one” and inserted requirement relating to residency of each member in cl. (3).Pub. L. 94–398

children.heading: Establishment; duties; termination; membership; term of members; appointment; Chairman
children.label: a Establishment; duties; termination; membership; term of members; appointment; Chairman

children.text: one member from each county in which the seashore is located, to be selected from recommendations made by the county commission in each county;
two members representing the State of Florida who shall be selected from recommendations made by the Governor of Florida; and
two members representing the general public: , That one member shall be appointed from each county in which the seashore is located.Provided

children.heading: Meetings; vacancies
children.label: b Meetings; vacancies
children.text: After the Secretary designates the member to be Chairman, the Commission may meet as often as necessary at the call of the Chairman or of the Secretary, or upon petition of a majority of the members of the Commission. Any vacancy in the Commission shall be filled in the same manner as the original appointment was made.

children.heading: Compensation; payment of expenses upon vouchers
children.label: c Compensation; payment of expenses upon vouchers
children.text: Members of the Commission shall serve without compensation, as such, but the Secretary may pay, upon vouchers signed by the Chairman, the expenses reasonably incurred by the Commission and its members in carrying out their responsibilities under this section.
